title: Setting up and Assigning Tax Rates

export_view.value:
Managing taxes is a crucial aspect of running a successful retail business. RepairQ assigns tax rates at the Item Type level, ensuring that all items within a designated category are taxed accurately when added to a ticket.

It's essential to have the correct tax information for your area, so if you have any questions or concerns regarding specific tax rates, we recommend consulting with your tax professional or accountant. Once you have the necessary information, our tutorial will guide you through adding and configuring taxes within RepairQ, ensuring compliance and accuracy in your transactions.

Next Steps and Additional Resources

  • Customizing Ticket Item Columns and Totals

  • Invoice Templates: GST and Tax-Inclusive Settings

  • Tax-Inclusive Pricing: Location Setup

  • Tax-Inclusive Pricing: GST Overview

  • Reports: Taxes

  • Creating a New Item Type

  • Tax Calculations for Bundled Items

Establishing Taxes for your Location(s)

There are three categories of taxes in RepairQ: Tax Groups, Tax Classes, and Tax Rates. Groups and Classes can be created and managed at the Company (global) or Location levels. Tax Rates are applied specifically at the Location level and can be copied to new locations that follow the same structure.

Create Tax Groups and Classes

On the Navigation Bar, go to Settings → Company Settings. On the Settings Overview page, you can find Tax Groups and Tax Classes under the Taxes category.

Tax Groups

The highest level of tax management in RepairQ is Tax Groups. Tax Groups determine how taxes are displayed on tickets, and reports are calculated based on each Tax Group.

To add a new Tax Group, click New Tax Group at the top right of the manage page. Alternatively, you can edit an existing Tax Group by clicking the Edit button next to the appropriate Tax Group.

Begin filling out the fields for the new Tax Group. Once you finish making changes, click Save to create/update the group.

  • Name - The Tax Group Name is displayed on receipts or invoices.

    • For example, locations using GST should name this field GST.

  • Location - An Admin can select whether this group will apply to all locations or a specific location.

  • Description - (Optional) This is additional information about the Tax Group to be tracked.

  • Active - Use this option to Activate/Deactivate the tax group.

Tax Classes

The second tier of tax management is Tax Classes. Classes define the tax type, tax rate, and which Tax Group to add the tax class amount into.

In this setup, we can appropriately set the different Tax Rates for Merchandise and Services and create an easy-to-understand Tax Report since both Tax Classes will aggregate under the Sales Tax Group.

To add a new Tax Class, click Add Tax Class at the top right of the manage page. Alternatively, you can edit an existing class by clicking the Edit button next to the appropriate Tax Class.

Begin filling out the fields for the new Tax Class. Once you finish making your changes, click Save to create/update the class.

  • Name - Assign a name to the Tax Class. For Tax Groups that have one child Tax Class, this can match the name of the parent Tax Group to avoid confusion.

  • Location - An Admin can select whether this group will apply to all locations or a specific location.

  • Type - Select whether this is treated as a Tax Rate or Surcharge.

  • Percentage - This field will calculate the percentage of tax applied when assigned to an Item Type.

  • Tax Group - Select the parent Tax Group for this tax.

  • Active - Use this option to Activate/Deactivate the tax group.

Create Tax Rates

Tax Rates are assigned Tax Classes (tax type and rate) to specific Item Types at specific Locations. To record and collect taxes on items/services, rates must be assigned to an Item Type. If you have additional locations with identical tax rates, they can be copied from an established location.

Note: Do not assign a tax rate if you have a non-taxable item type. Assigning a 0% tax rate flags the item as taxable at a 0% rate.

Changes to Tax Rates will not retroactively change the tax amounts for items currently on tickets or Closed tickets. However, when a ticket is edited after making a tax rate change, the tax amount will be recalculated according to the new settings.

On the Navigation Bar, go to Settings → Location Settings. On the Settings Overview page, scroll to the Taxes section and click Tax Rates.

To assign a new Tax Rate, click Add Tax Rate at the top right of the page or click the Add button next to the corresponding Item Type. You can edit an existing rate applied to an Item Type by clicking the Edit button next to the appropriate Item Type.

Note: Tax Rate assignments can be deleted by clicking the Delete button next to the Item Type.

Begin filling out the fields for the new Tax Rate. Once you make your changes, click Save to update the Item Type with the new rate.

  • Item Type - This field selects which Item Type this rate is applied to.

  • Location - This field selects which location the Tax Rate is applied to. Rates cannot be applied to all locations at once.

  • Tax Class - This field selects which Tax Class will be applied to the Item Type.

  • Tax when bundled? - Select this field to apply taxes when this item is bundled as the child item. For more information, see our article Catalog Items: Bundle Relationships, Auto-Bundling, and Auto-Adding Items. If you are unsure about the tax requirements for bundled items, we recommend consulting a CPA on proper policies.

  • Bundled tax basis - This field controls whether this tax applied is based on the cost or price of the item. For more information, see Tax Calculations for Bundled Items.

  • Active - Select whether to activate/deactivate the applied tax for this item type.

Once you have saved your selections, the Tax Rate list will update the Item Type with the assigned Tax Class, Type, Rate %, and other details. These steps will need to be repeated for each Item Type that will be taxed.

Copy Tax Rates

If you have established tax rates for an existing location and would like to assign the same rates for a secondary location or overwrite the tax rate assignment from another location. In that case, you can click Copy Tax Rates From Another Location. This will remove any applied rates to the current location and replace them from the selected location.

START 1.22.X
